subject: mathematics
title: Grade 4 Mathematics
description: Fractions, decimals, area and perimeter, and multi-digit multiplication.

lessons.title: Understanding Fractions
lessons.objective: Learn what fractions are and how to compare them.
lessons.content:
A fraction represents a part of a whole. It has two numbers:

• The NUMERATOR (top number) tells how many parts you have.
• The DENOMINATOR (bottom number) tells how many equal parts the whole is divided into.

For example, 3/4 means you have 3 out of 4 equal parts.

Comparing fractions with the SAME denominator is easy — just compare the numerators!
  2/5 < 4/5 because 2 < 4

When denominators are DIFFERENT, find a common denominator first:
  1/3 vs 1/4 → 4/12 vs 3/12 → 1/3 is bigger!

exercises.question: What does the denominator of a fraction tell you?
options: How many equal parts the whole is divided into
exercises.correctAnswer: How many equal parts the whole is divided into
exercises.hint: Look at the bottom number — it's about the whole.
exercises.explanation: The denominator (bottom number) tells you how many equal parts the whole is divided into. For example, in 3/4, the 4 means the whole is split into 4 equal pieces.

exercises.question: Which fraction is larger: 3/8 or 5/8?
exercises.hint: When denominators are the same, compare the numerators.
exercises.explanation: Both fractions have denominator 8, so we just compare numerators: 5 > 3, so 5/8 > 3/8.

exercises.question: Write the fraction for 'two out of five parts': (write as a/b)
exercises.hint: The numerator is how many parts you have, the denominator is the total parts.
exercises.explanation: Two parts out of five means the numerator is 2 and the denominator is 5, giving us 2/5.

lessons.title: Introduction to Decimals
lessons.objective: Understand the relationship between fractions and decimals.
lessons.content:
Decimals are another way to write fractions!

The decimal point separates whole numbers from parts:
  3.5 means 3 and 5 tenths (3 + 5/10)
  0.25 means 25 hundredths (25/100 = 1/4)

Place values after the decimal:
  • First place: TENTHS (1/10)
  • Second place: HUNDREDTHS (1/100)
  • Third place: THOUSANDTHS (1/1000)

Converting fractions to decimals:
  1/2 = 0.5
  1/4 = 0.25
  3/4 = 0.75

exercises.question: What is 1/4 as a decimal?
exercises.explanation: 1/4 = 1 ÷ 4 = 0.25. You can also think of it as 25 out of 100 pennies in a pound.

exercises.question: What is 0.5 as a fraction? (write in simplest form as a/b)

lessons.title: Area and Perimeter
lessons.objective: Calculate the area and perimeter of rectangles.
lessons.content:
PERIMETER is the distance around a shape.
For a rectangle: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width)

AREA is the space inside a shape.
For a rectangle: Area = length × width

Example: A rectangle with length 6 cm and width 4 cm:
  Perimeter = 2 × (6 + 4) = 2 × 10 = 20 cm
  Area = 6 × 4 = 24 cm²

Remember: Perimeter is measured in cm, m, etc.
          Area is measured in cm², m², etc. (squared units!)

exercises.question: What is the area of a rectangle with length 8 cm and width 5 cm?
exercises.explanation: Area = 8 × 5 = 40 cm². Remember to use squared units for area!

exercises.question: A rectangle has length 10 m and width 3 m. What is its perimeter in metres? (number only)
